2604.25521.title: Automated Adversarial Collaboration for Advancing Theory Building in the Cognitive Sciences
2604.25521.paper_summary: Cognitive science often evaluates theories through narrow paradigms and local model comparisons, limiting the integration of evidence across tasks and realizations. We introduce an automated adversarial collaboration framework for adjudicating among competing theories even when the candidate models and experiments must be discovered during the adjudication process. The system combines LLM-based theory agents, program synthesis, and information-theoretic experimental design in a closed loop. In a simulation study spanning three classic categorization theories, the framework recovered the ground-truth theory across noise settings with weaker reliability in the hardest settings. Together, the framework and findings provide a concrete proof of concept for closed-loop, in-silico theory adjudication in cognitive science.
2604.25521.paper_summary_zh: 認知科學經常通過狹窄的範式和局部模型比較來評估理論，這限制了在任務和實現之間整合證據的能力。<lb/>我們引入了一個自動化的對抗性合作框架，用於在競爭理論之間進行裁定，即使候選模型和實驗必須在裁定過程中被發現。<lb/>該系統結合了基於LLM的理論代理、程序合成和信息論實驗設計，形成一個閉環。<lb/>在一項涵蓋三個經典分類理論的模擬研究中，該框架在噪聲設置下恢復了真實理論，在最困難的設置中可靠性較弱。<lb/>總體而言，該框架和研究結果為認知科學中的閉環、計算機內理論裁定提供了具體的概念證明。
2604.25521.author: Suyog Chandramouli et.al.
2604.25521.authors: Suyog Chandramouli, George Kachergis, Akshay Jagadish
